docs.title: SecuredMe Education
docs.text:
SecuredMe Education is a public learning suite for students, teachers, and creators who want practical tools, visible progress, and a calmer way to begin.

Start With AlgoQuest Tool Index 40 Starter Prompts Notebook / Colab Access Console

docs.title: What This Suite IsStart SmallUse A PromptOpen A PathAdjust Comfort
docs.text:
Learn

Pick one tool and one activity.

Connect

Turn a question into a result.

Explore

Move from idea to tool to artifact.

Access

Use calmer reading and focus settings.

docs.title: First ToolAlgoQuest Discovery Labs
docs.text:
Tool 01

Play with algorithms through guided challenges, everyday examples, and visual checkpoints.

Open Tool Page Use A Starter Prompt Open Notebook

docs.title: Start In 15 Minutes
docs.text: Step Action Link 1 Pick one everyday task. Open AlgoQuest 2 Turn the task into five steps. Prompt Matrix 3 Draw a simple flow: input, action, result. Tool Index 4 Keep the clearest result and choose one next move. Access Console

docs.title: Suite Index
docs.text:
The full suite stays available, but the recommended first path is AlgoQuest.

Tool Best First Use AlgoQuest Discovery Labs Play with algorithms through guided challenges and visual checkpoints. Algorithm Builder Turn a learning idea into a clear step-by-step plan. CeLeBrUm Learning Lab Plan goals, organize reflection, and turn study momentum into next steps. FFeD QLC Explore quality, logic, and learning checks through structured activities. FNP-QNN MVP Explore quantum-inspired learning models with visual, classroom-friendly prompts. Hippo Memory Learning Study how memory-inspired learning can support research and review. Market Guardian Understand retail safety, stock awareness, and practical decision checks. QuaNThoR Explore quantum-inspired reasoning through accessible learning experiments. SCL License Read the shared education license language for the suite. SecuredMe Scholarium Support classroom learning paths, teacher review, and student progress. Synthia Guide learning conversations, reflection, and clear next actions. Tesla Recovery Workbench Study invention, recovery, and experiment planning through guided activities. V.O.T Guardian Practice visual observation and trust thinking with learner-friendly examples. Visual Algorithm Designer Sketch algorithm steps visually before turning them into implementation tasks.

docs.title: Visual Observation Practice
docs.text: Separate what you see from what you infer. Then explain what evidence would make the result more trustworthy.

docs.title: Access Console
docs.text: The SecuredMe Education site includes two public support controls for readers.

docs.title: Dark / Light Mode
docs.text:
Use the Dark / Light button in the upper-right header to switch the whole site between the Education light and dark themes.

This button is separate from the Access console. It keeps the selected visual mode while users move between pages.

docs.title: Access Console
docs.text:
Use the Access button in the lower-right corner, or press Ctrl+U, to open the Access console.

The console is designed for edge users, neurodivergent students, hyperactive readers, teachers, older readers, and anyone who needs a calmer first week with the site. Settings stay in the current browser.

docs.title: Keyboard Controls
docs.text: Shortcut Action Ctrl+U Open or close the Access console. Escape Close the console. Ctrl+Alt+S Start or stop the sprint timer.

docs.title: Profiles
docs.text: Profile Purpose Teacher Focus Stronger link visibility, readable font, reduced motion, and current-section support. Low Vision Larger text, stronger contrast, smart contrast, and oversized controls. Dyslexia Support Readable font, wider spacing, horizontal reading pacer, and content chunking. Motion Safe Reduced motion, predictable transitions, and stronger panel contrast. Neuro Focus Focus frame, quiet navigation, chunked content, and current-section highlight. ADHD Sprint Focus frame, sprint rhythm, micro-breaks, low-demand mode, and reading pacer. Autism Calm Predictable mode, literal labels, calm layout, stimulus control, reduced motion, and quiet navigation. Sensory Safe Calm layout, stimulus control, reduced motion, smart contrast, and low-demand mode. Deep Work / Hyperfocus Focus frame, quiet navigation, current section, high-agency density, and 25-minute sprint length.

docs.title: Public Note
docs.text: This console is a reader-support layer, not a legal compliance certificate, medical tool, or automated remediation service. It is here to help readers adjust the experience to their comfort.

docs.title: Neurodivergent Comfort
docs.text:
SecuredMe Education treats accessibility as a public learning surface. The Access console supports comfort, attention, predictability, and user agency for neurodivergent readers without making medical claims.

The design follows public accessibility guidance: clear purpose, understandable structure, focus support, reduced memory burden, predictable navigation, and personalization. These controls support comfort; they do not replace accessibility review or user testing.

docs.title: Comfort Options
docs.text: Need Helpful feature Reduce distraction while keeping controls reachable Focus Frame Keep navigation predictable but less visually loud Quiet Nav Reduce sensory overload Calm Layout Reduce aggressive saturation Stimulus Control Help readers parse long pages Content Chunking Help users know where they are Current Section Support dyslexia and attention tracking Reading Pacer Support autism and anxiety through stable UI Predictable Mode Avoid metaphor-heavy labeling Literal Labels Help technical vocabulary discovery Glossary Boost Turn reading into visible progress Checklist Mode Mark progress in a familiar way Done Marker Reduce memory burden Resume Point Support ADHD activation Sprint Timer Support transition out of hyperfocus Micro Breaks Reduce pressure for anxious readers Low Demand Mode Support expert readers High Agency Mode Support motor comfort Oversized Targets+

docs.title: Profiles
docs.text: Profile Best first use Neuro Focus A reader gets distracted by navigation or decorative surfaces. ADHD Sprint A reader needs short activation loops, a timer, and a visible next step. Autism Calm A reader needs predictable labels, fewer stimuli, and low surprise. Sensory Safe A reader is overloaded by motion, saturation, or high-energy visuals. Deep Work / Hyperfocus A high-agency reader wants a quieter environment for long technical work.

docs.title: Quick Check
docs.text:
• Open Access with the launcher and Ctrl+U.
  • Confirm Escape closes the panel.
  • Apply a profile and verify the page feels more comfortable.
  • Start and stop the sprint timer with the button and Ctrl+Alt+S.
  • Confirm dark/light mode remains separate from Access.
  • Confirm mobile layout has no overlap and targets remain usable.

docs.title: Public Boundary
docs.text: These notebooks are learning worksheets. They do not need account keys, special setup, or private project details.

docs.title: Onboarding
docs.text: This onboarding path helps a new user understand the tool in their hands without needing to know how the system is built.

docs.title: Pick Your TrackI Need To StartI Need A Class ActivityI Need To BuildI Need Less Friction
docs.text:
Student

Use prompts 01 to 10 to understand the suite, choose a tool, and create a small first result.

Teacher

Use prompts 07, 09, 24, 25, 26, 32, and 38 to prepare classroom material and feedback.

Creator

Use prompts 11, 16, 18, 31, 35, 36, 39, and 40 to turn an idea into a visible artifact.

Comfort

Use prompts 14, 20, 21, 28, and 34, then open Access if reading or focus needs adjustment.

docs.title: First 15 Minutes
docs.text: Minute Action Result 0-2 Choose one track above. You know where to start. 2-5 Open the 40-prompt matrix and pick one prompt. You have a clear request ready. 5-10 Replace the topic with your real learning goal. The prompt belongs to your situation. 10-14 Read the answer and keep the useful part. You have a first artifact. 14-15 Choose the next action. Progress continues instead of stopping.

docs.title: Notebook Path
docs.text:
Use the notebook version when you want the prompts in a worksheet format. The notebook works as a guided learning canvas:

  1. open the notebook;
  2. choose a prompt number;
  3. add your topic, audience, and wanted result;
  4. copy the prepared prompt;
  5. paste the result back into your notes.

Open Notebook / Colab for the Jupyter file and the Colab link.

docs.title: How To Use A Prompt
docs.text:
Copy the prompt, then add three details:

  • your topic
  • your audience
  • the result you want

Example:

Create a 15 minute first activity using one SecuredMe Education tool.
Topic: visual algorithms
Audience: high school beginners
Result wanted: a small worksheet with one example and one reflection question
Use simple words, show the expected result, and finish with one next action.

docs.title: Good First Outputs
docs.text: Output When To Use It Checklist When the learner needs visible progress. Worksheet When a teacher needs classroom material. Comparison table When the learner must choose between options. Reflection note When the learner needs to understand what changed. Build steps When the result should become a small project.

docs.title: Next
docs.text: Open 40 Starter Prompts, choose one prompt, and make your first artifact. Use the Notebook / Colab version if you prefer a worksheet.

docs.title: 40 Starter Prompts
docs.text:
These prompts help beginners use SecuredMe Education without needing to know how the system is built. Copy one prompt, replace the topic with your own goal, and keep the result.

Prompt 01

Understand the suite

Objective: Explain SecuredMe Education in simple words for a new learner.

Prompt: Explain SecuredMe Education in simple words for a new learner. Use simple words, show the expected result, and finish with one next action.

Expected result: A clear beginner-friendly output that can be used right away.

Next action: Save the best part and try one small improvement.

Prompt page Notebook Open in Colab

Prompt 02

Choose my first tool

Objective: Help me choose the best SecuredMe Education tool for my current learning goal.

Prompt: Help me choose the best SecuredMe Education tool for my current learning goal. Use simple words, show the expected result, and finish with one next action.

Expected result: A clear beginner-friendly output that can be used right away.

Next action: Save the best part and try one small improvement.

docs.title: Tools
docs.text: Each page below explains what the tool is for, who it helps, and how to start with one practical activity.

docs.title: Start HereAlgoQuest Discovery LabsAlgoQuest Discovery LabsAlgorithm BuilderCeLeBrUm Learning LabFFeD QLCFNP-QNN MVPHippo Memory LearningMarket GuardianQuaNThoRSCL LicenseSecuredMe ScholariumSynthiaTesla Recovery WorkbenchV.O.T GuardianVisual Algorithm Designer
docs.text:
Tool 01

Begin here if you want a friendly first activity: choose a task, split it into steps, and keep a small visible result.

Open AlgoQuest Choose A Prompt

Education Tool

Play with algorithms through guided challenges and visual checkpoints.

Education Tool

Turn a learning idea into a clear step-by-step plan.

Education Tool

Plan goals, organize reflection, and turn study momentum into next steps.

Education Tool

Explore quality, logic, and learning checks through structured activities.

Education Tool

Explore quantum-inspired learning models with visual, classroom-friendly prompts.

Education Tool

Study how memory-inspired learning can support research and review.

Education Tool

Understand retail safety, stock awareness, and practical decision checks.

Education Tool

Explore quantum-inspired reasoning through accessible learning experiments.

Education Tool

Read the shared education license language for the suite.

Education Tool

Support classroom learning paths, teacher review, and student progress.

Education Tool

Guide learning conversations, reflection, and clear next actions.

Education Tool

Study invention, recovery, and experiment planning through guided activities.

Education Tool

Practice visual observation and trust thinking with learner-friendly examples.

Education Tool
